01_initial_request.target_name: Nit1 and Fhit tumor suppressor proteins
01_initial_request.input_data: Purified recombinant human Nit1 and Fhit proteins
01_initial_request.user_intent: To test the Rosetta Stone hypothesis by providing biochemical evidence for a direct association between Nit1 and Fhit
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Biochemical evidence of direct physical association and identification of common interaction partners

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] The Rosetta Stone hypothesis predicts that proteins expressed as fusion proteins in some organisms should interact when expressed separately in others. Nit1 and Fhit are expressed as NitFhit fusion in D. melanogaster and C. elegans. [Gap] Co-evolution relationship between Nit1 and Fhit needs to be established. [Decision] To achieve phylogenetic analysis, chose to use Epos 0.92svn (Blast-module) to perform sequence search on NitFhit fusion protein, nitrilase domain, and Fhit domain, expecting to obtain homologous sequences across species.

parameters.search_template: entire fusion protein, nitrilase domain, Fhit domain

02_agent_trajectory.observation: 19 fusion proteins, 18 Fhit, and 46 nitrilases sequences were retrieved

references.citation: Griebel, T., et al. EPoS: a modular software framework for phylogenetic analysis. Bioinformatics 2008
references.relevance: Provides the phylogenetic analysis tool used for sequence search

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Sequences were retrieved from Blast search. [Gap] Sequences need to be aligned for phylogenetic analysis. [Decision] To achieve sequence alignment, chose to use Epos (T-Coffee module) to perform alignment on nitrilases plus nitrilase domain of fusion proteins, expecting to obtain aligned sequences for tree construction.

parameters.alignment_target: nitrilases plus nitrilase domain of fusion proteins

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Sequences were aligned for phylogenetic tree reconstruction

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Sequences were aligned. [Gap] Appropriate substitution model for phylogenetic tree reconstruction was not known. [Decision] To achieve model selection, chose to use ProtTest 2.4 to perform substitution model testing, expecting to obtain the most appropriate substitution model.

parameters.result: LG matrix most appropriate, followed by WAG and JTT matrix

02_agent_trajectory.observation: LG matrix identified as most appropriate substitution model

references.citation: Abascal, F., et al. ProtTest: selection of best-fit models of protein evolution. Bioinformatics 2005
references.relevance: Provides the model selection tool used

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Substitution model was selected. [Gap] Phylogenetic tree needs to be constructed. [Decision] To achieve phylogenetic tree construction, chose to use Epos (RAxML modul) to perform maximum likelihood analysis with WAG matrix, expecting to obtain phylogenetic tree.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Phylogenetic tree constructed using maximum likelihood approach

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Maximum likelihood tree was constructed. [Gap] Methodological bias needs to be minimized. [Decision] To achieve Bayesian phylogenetic analysis, chose to use MrBayes model to perform Bayesian approach, expecting to obtain complementary phylogenetic tree.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Phylogenetic tree constructed using Bayesian approach

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Phylogenetic analysis suggested co-evolution of Nit1 and Fhit. [Gap] Direct biochemical evidence of interaction is needed. [Decision] To achieve protein production, chose to use E. coli expression system (XL1 blue or BL21DE3) to express GST- and MBP-fusion proteins, expecting to obtain recombinant proteins for interaction studies.

parameters.induction_time: 1 h at 37°C or overnight at 17°C for GST-TEV-Nit1

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Fusion proteins were expressed in E. coli

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Fusion proteins were expressed. [Gap] Proteins need to be purified for interaction assays. [Decision] To achieve GST-fusion protein purification, chose to use GSH-agarose beads to perform affinity purification, expecting to obtain purified GST-fusion proteins.

parameters.elution: 20 mM glutathione in 40 mM Tris/HCl pH 8.0, 100 mM NaCl

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] GST-fusion proteins were purified. [Gap] MBP-fusion proteins also need purification. [Decision] To achieve MBP-fusion protein purification, chose to use amylose resin to perform affinity purification, expecting to obtain purified MBP-fusion proteins.

parameters.elution: 20 mM maltose in 40 mM Tris/HCl pH 8.0, 100 mM NaCl

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Fusion proteins were purified. [Gap] Untagged proteins needed for SEC analysis. [Decision] To achieve tag removal, chose to use TEV protease to perform on-column cleavage of GST tag, expecting to obtain untagged Nit1 and Fhit proteins.

parameters.buffer: 50 mM Tris/HCl pH 8.0, 100 mM NaCl, 2 mM MgCl2

02_agent_trajectory.observation: GST tag was removed from Nit1 and Fhit proteins

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Untagged proteins were obtained. [Gap] Oligomeric structure of Nit1 needs to be determined. [Decision] To achieve oligomerization analysis, chose to use Superdex™200-HR column to perform size exclusion chromatography, expecting to determine molecular mass and oligomeric state.

parameters.buffer: 50 mM Tris/HCl pH 8.0, 100 mM NaCl, 2 mM MgCl2
parameters.standards: β-galactosidase (464 kDa), catalase (232 kDa), BSA (67 kDa), ovalbumin (47 kDa), carboanhydrase (29 kDa), lysozyme (14 kDa)

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Nit1 eluted at ~127 kDa (tetramer), Fhit eluted at ~43 kDa (dimer)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Oligomeric states were determined. [Gap] Complex formation in cells needs to be tested. [Decision] To achieve co-immunoprecipitation, chose to use Protein A Sepharose™ with anti-myc/anti-FLAG antibodies to perform immunoprecipitation from HEK-293 cell lysates, expecting to detect Nit1-Fhit complex.

parameters.lysis_buffer: 20 mM imidazole pH 8.0, 150 mM NaCl, 2 mM MgCl2, 300 mM sucrose, 0.25% Triton X-100

02_agent_trajectory.observation: FLAG-Nit1 specifically associated with Fhit-myc6; Nit1ΔC did not associate; Nit1C167A showed similar association as wild-type

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Co-IP showed association in cell lysates. [Gap] Direct interaction needs to be confirmed with purified proteins. [Decision] To achieve direct interaction test, chose to use GSH-agarose beads to perform GST pull-down assays with purified recombinant proteins, expecting to confirm direct Nit1-Fhit binding.

parameters.buffer: 150 mM NaCl, 20 mM imidazole pH 8.0, 2 mM MgCl2, 300 mM sucrose, 0.25% Triton X-100 or 20 mM Tris/HCl pH 7.5, 150 mM NaCl, 2 mM MgCl2, 300 mM sucrose, 0.25% Triton X-100
parameters.proteins_tested: GST-Fhit with MBP-Nit1, GST-dNit with MBP-dFhit, GST-FhitH96N with MBP-Nit1

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MBP-Nit1 directly associates with GST-Fhit; C-terminally deleted Nit1ΔC did not interact; enzymatic inactive variants still bound

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Direct interaction was confirmed with purified proteins. [Gap] Endogenous interaction in cells needs to be detected. [Decision] To achieve endogenous interaction detection, chose to use Duolink® Assay to perform proximity ligation assay in MCF-7 cells, expecting to detect Nit1-Fhit proximity in situ.

parameters.cell_type: MCF-7 shNit1 knock-down clone 12 or scrambled clone

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Typical spotlike signals for endogenous Nit1-Fhit interaction detected; shNit1 knock-down showed significantly lower signals

references.citation: Söderberg, O., et al. Direct observation of individual endogenous protein complexes in situ by proximity ligation. Nat. Methods 2006

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Nit1-Fhit interaction was confirmed. [Gap] Common interaction partners need to be identified per Rosetta Stone hypothesis. [Decision] To achieve common partner identification, chose to use GSH-agarose beads to perform pull-down assays with GST-Ubc9 and GST-Hsp60, expecting to identify shared binding partners.

parameters.proteins_tested: GST-mUbc9 or GST-Hsp60 with MBP-Nit1 or MBP-Fhit

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Both Nit1 and Fhit associated with Ubc9 and Hsp60

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] All interaction assays were performed. [Gap] Protein detection and verification needed throughout experiments. [Decision] To achieve protein detection, chose to use Western blot analysis with various antibodies to visualize protein interactions, expecting to confirm all binding results.

parameters.antibodies: anti-FLAG M2 (1 μg/mL), anti-myc 9E10 (1:1,000), anti-GST, anti-MBP, anti-Fhit (G4), anti-Nit1

02_agent_trajectory.observation: All protein interactions were visualized and confirmed by Western blot

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Multiple biochemical approaches including co-immunoprecipitation, in vitro pull-down assays with purified recombinant proteins, size exclusion chromatography, and proximity ligation assays

nit1_oligomeric_state.interpretation: Nit1 forms tetramers as predicted by Rosetta Stone hypothesis (monomer ~32 kDa)

fhit_oligomeric_state.interpretation: Fhit forms dimers (monomer ~17 kDa), consistent with literature

direct_interaction.interpretation: MBP-Nit1 directly associates with GST-Fhit in pull-down assays

c_terminal_requirement.interpretation: C-terminal 13 amino acids of Nit1 are necessary for Fhit interaction

endogenous_interaction.interpretation: Spotlike PLA signals detected in MCF-7 cells; significantly reduced in shNit1 knock-down

common_partners.interpretation: Hsp60 and Ubc9 identified as common interaction partners of both Nit1 and Fhit

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved. The study provides biochemical evidence for direct physical association between Nit1 and Fhit tumor suppressor proteins, confirming the Rosetta Stone hypothesis prediction. Additionally, common interaction partners (Hsp60 and Ubc9) were identified, and Nit1 was shown to form tetramers similar to the NitFhit fusion protein structure in C. elegans.
